observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee and all other concerned Municipal Officials to:
a. Regularly monitor the Municipality’s cash position and ensure availability of cash before incurring obligations;
b. Exercise prudence in the use of the limited cash resources by implementing cost-cutting measures and avoiding unnecessary expenses; and
c. Continue to undertake revenue-enhancing strategies to generate additional income and strengthen the collections of its receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality lessened the cash overdraft by P14,201,062.18, as of December 31, 2024. They are undertaking various financial measures to avoid cash overdraft in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resources Management Office and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Formulate policies, guidelines and internal control to ensure that all employees of the Municipality will receive at least the mandatory net take home pay of P5,000.00 as prescribed by law; and
b. Exercise diligence in the payroll preparation such as strict monitoring of the net compensation received by each employee and if warranted, remedial measures should be placed to correct the poor condition of the 15 employees receiving an amount below the minimum net take home pay.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will issue an order instructing the concerned department heads to strictly monitor and limit the loans allowed to its employees to ensure that the minimum required net take home pay is complied with.
observations.reason: 31 employees of the Municipality incurred net take home pay less than the statutory monthly of P5,000.00 in CY 2024. Reiterated with modification in Audit Observation No. 18, Page Nos. 86 to 87, Part II of this Report. For preparation of RTS for recommendation considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned to:
a. Submit the Monthly Audit Report on Fuel Consumption and strictly adhere to the guidelines, rules and regulations of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 to prevent unnecessary and excessive consumption of the gasoline;
b. Stop granting gasoline subsidies to private individuals and/or privately owned motor vehicles and submit legal basis supporting the program/activity of the Municipality on the granting gasoline subsidies to private individuals and/or privately owned motor vehicles; otherwise the paid transactions will be disallowed in audit; and
c. Submit written explanation/justification and other supporting documents on charging fuel expenses under Market Administration Market Fund and Special Education Fund (SEF) without appropriations, otherwise the paid transactions will be disallowed in audit; and
d. Strictly monitor the funds available for the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants to avoid overspending and refrain from charging the same against the current appropriations. Moreover, record the expenses following the accrual method of accounting.

The Municipal Budget Officer submitted to the Audit Team an explanation supported with copies of the SEF budget.

within 30 days after return to the permanent official station in the case of local
travel to prevent the granting of additional cash advance to any officer or
employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance; and
d. ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant
to COA Circular No. 97-002.

replacement of the subject checks one month before it became stale; and

checks by debiting Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and
crediting Accounts Payable accounts both by P90,497.96.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. ensure the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers, particularly those accounts
enumerated under Section 4(e) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I showing the
detailed breakdown of the transactions; and

to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide
them with additional information value.

disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details as to the amount available,
utilized and the unexpended balances as of year-end as presented under COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the former RCC the immediate
restitution of the remaining unremitted collections of P136,938.00, without prejudice
to the filing of appropriate legal action.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
implement the following internal control mechanisms:

a. direct the assigned Liquidating Officer to verify the details of the collections
reflected in the duplicate and triplicate copies forwarded to him/her before
recording to the RCD;
b. regularly send monthly billing statements to the market stallholders indicating
thereto the amount due and period covered to serve as a confirmation letter in
order to prevent the tampering of ORs;
c. enforce close supervision of personnel by reviewing/checking and monitoring
the issued ORs as to series numbers, dates and total collections remitted by the
RCC as reflected in his/her RCR before recording/posting in the cashbook to
prevent the non-remittance of collections; and
d. revisit its internal control over cash and strengthen the identified weakness.

Committee, direct the:

P953,589.17 to the nine defaulting stallholders, otherwise, request to the Market

b. Market Committee to strictly implement the policy on the automatic cancellation

consecutive months to prevent the accumulation of uncollected rental fees and
to allow other stall applicants the opportunity to conduct business within the
market premises.

unutilized balance of LDRRMF between the Statement of Financial Position and

adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate refund of the excess claim of traveling and training expenses and strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298.

We recommended the Management to: • justify why the payment for consultancy services shall not be disallowed in audit for failure of the procuring entity to comply with the prescribed mode of procurement mandated by RA No. 9184; • abide by the provisions of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of consulting services and adopt written policy/ procedure/ guidelines in hiring consultants to ensure that only the services of individuals who have technical and professional expertise on the types of projects or activities needed by the Municipality would be availed of; • evaluate and streamline the current roster of consultants and discontinue renewing consultants with redundant responsibilities to prevent unnecessary and excessive expenditures; and • perform periodic monitoring/oversight in the hiring of consultants to determine the extent to which economy, efficiency and effectiveness have been achieved.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted a justification letter dated June 15, 2016. The Management partially complied with the recommendation. The Management evaluated the roster of consultants. The number of employed consultants was significantly lowered. Accomplishment reports were duly attached together with the Disbursement Vouchers.
observations.reason: Related audit issue is discussed in Observation No. 11, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the concerned officials of the Municipality to: • include in the Consultancy Contracts clear and detailed statements of the consultant’s responsibilities to give assurance that their scope of work are neither duplication of works of incumbent Municipal officials nor beyond the in-house capability of the Municipality to undertake and to substantiate whether the purpose for which they were hired was achieved; • exercise prudence in the disbursement/disposition of government funds and avoid processing of payments with incomplete documentation/attachments; and • reassess the need of hiring consultants and determine their capabilities based on proven expertise, experience and capability to avoid unnecessary and excessive expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the copy of the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit on the hiring of the legal counsel thru consultancy contract and/or other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted a justification letter dated June 15, 2016.
observations.reason: Management is considering to seek concurrence of COA on the hiring of legal consultant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculture Office to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office and submit schedules, data and other necessary documents that can assist the latter in determining the current value of the breeding stocks. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw adjusting entries to adjust the balance of the breeding stock upon the submission of the necessary documents that will support its current value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG to submit the GAD reports together with the required documents to Office of the Auditor for evaluation and strictly comply with the PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor fully comply with the requirement of Section 4.1A.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and create the LGU-GFPS in accordance with the structure and composition provided for in the Magna Carta for Women-Implementing Rules and Regulations i.e. the Local Government Unit GAD Focal Point System shall have an Executive Committee headed by the Local Chief Executive, a Technical Working Group and/or a Secretariat. The LCE should sign an appropriate issuance for the creation of GFPS by issuing office order/appointments or passed a Sanggunian Bayan Resolution/Ordinance creating the same. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor pursue the establishment of the Local GAD Code by issuing the instruction to the LGU GFPS to assist the Sanggunian in the formulation of the Local GAD Code. We recommended that the LGU GFPS formulate measures that will ensure establishment of the Local Government Code of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor pursue the establishment of the Local GAD Code by issuing the instruction to the LGU GFPS to assist the Sanggunian in the formulation of the Local GAD Code. We also recommended that the LGU GFPS formulate measures that will ensure establishment of the Local Government Code of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government fully comply with the requirement of Sec. 11 of RA No. 10121 otherwise known as “The Philippine Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2012” and create LDRRMC by issuing an office order/appointments or passed a Sanggunian Pambayan Resolution/Ordinance e creating the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor form or establish a complete set of Solid Waste Management Board pursuant to Section 12 of RA No. 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Management Act of 2000 for effective formulation and implementation of the Municipality's Solid Waste Management Plan. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to conduct regular meetings to ensure that the Component Barangays are one with the Municipality in the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plans and to immediately act in case of problems encountered in its implementation. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO Head to continue the strict implementation of the segregation at source currently implemented by the Municipality and devise more effective and efficient measures that will ensure the waste reduction of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipality continue their efforts to adhere with their plan of completely shutting down the operations of their controlled dumpsites by the end of 2016 and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with the recommendation and created a Solid Waste Management Board. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board conducted regular meetings. The Management forwarded the necessary documents to the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) in November 2016 for the closure of the controlled dumpsite.
